content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 18,
1992
Susan Bott, Treasurer
Committee to Re—Elect Bill Wallace
p. o.
Box 2483
Goleta,
CA
93118
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-92-540
Dear Ms.
Bott:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
!
Because
your letter states only a general question,
we consider it to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) 2/
QUESTION
You have asked for guidance in conducting fundraising
concerts in order to comply with the provisions of the Act.
DISCUSSION
Absent specific information concerning the concerts,
we can
provide only general guidance.
Persons who purchase tickets to a
concert which is conducted as a political fundraiser for a
particular candidate or committee are deemed to be making
contributions to the candidate or committee in the amount of the
tickets purchased.
Under these circumstances, the candidate or
committee must comply with the campaign provisions of
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 j Street, Suite 8(X)
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(Qló’ 22-5660


Susan Bott
Our File No.
1—92—540
Page
2
the Act and Commission regulations concerning recordkeeping and
disclosure.
In addition,
cash contributions of $100 or more and
cash expenditures of $100 or more are prohibited.
The Act’s
provisions are discussed in detail in the Commission’s information
manuals.
I hope this information has been helpful.
If you have any
questions concerning this letter, please contact me at
(916)
322-
5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission December 18, 1992 Susan Bott, Treasurer Committee to Re—Elect Bill Wallace p. o. Box 2483 Goleta, CA 93118 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. 1-92-540 Dear Ms. Bott: You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 ! Because your letter states only a general question, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) 2/ QUESTION You have asked for guidance in conducting fundraising concerts in order to comply with the provisions of the Act. DISCUSSION Absent specific information concerning the concerts, we can provide only general guidance. Persons who purchase tickets to a concert which is conducted as a political fundraiser for a particular candidate or committee are deemed to be making contributions to the candidate or committee in the amount of the tickets purchased. Under these circumstances, the candidate or committee must comply with the campaign provisions of 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) 428 j Street, Suite 8(X) • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (Qló’ 22-5660 Susan Bott Our File No. 1—92—540 Page 2 the Act and Commission regulations concerning recordkeeping and disclosure. In addition, cash contributions of $100 or more and cash expenditures of $100 or more are prohibited. The Act’s provisions are discussed in detail in the Commission’s information manuals. I hope this information has been helpful. If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322- 5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Carla Wardlow Division Chief Technical Assistance Division
